result:
Scoped the project. Summary of what I returned:

- **targetStack:** AEM headless CMS with a React SPA consuming Content Fragments via GraphQL/Content Services.
- **domains:** Content Fragment modeling, AEM GraphQL API, Content Services/Assets HTTP API, React SPA architecture, the AEM headless JS client + editable-components SDK, SPA/Universal Editor, AEM project/Maven structure, auth & delivery, CORS/dispatcher/caching, and Cloud Manager deployment.
- **likelyTasks:** defining and exposing Content Fragment Models, writing/persisting GraphQL queries and fetching them in React, building CF-rendering components, integrating the `ui.frontend` build, configuring CORS/dispatcher, in-context editing, token auth across environments, and pipeline/SDK setup.
- **questions:** deployment target (AEMaaCS vs 6.5 vs local SDK), headless-only vs visual editing, delivery API choice, React hosting model, and auth approach — each materially changes which skills are needed.

The biggest forks for downstream research are **deployment target** (AEMaaCS vs 6.5 — different APIs, Universal Editor availability, pipeline tooling) and **editing model** (pure headless vs SPA/Universal Editor — entirely different SDK surface).

structured_output.targetStack: Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) headless CMS with a React SPA consuming Content Fragments via GraphQL/Content Services
domains: AEM Content Fragment Models & authoring (headless content modeling)
AEM GraphQL API (persisted queries, endpoints, filtering)
AEM Content Services / Assets HTTP API delivery
React SPA architecture (hooks, routing, data fetching)
AEM SPA Editor + Editable Components (Universal Editor / in-context editing)
AEM project structure & Maven archetype (ui.apps, ui.frontend, core bundles)
Authentication & delivery (dev tokens, AEM as a Cloud Service, Edge Delivery)
CORS, dispatcher config & caching for headless delivery
likelyTasks: Define a Content Fragment Model and expose it via a GraphQL endpoint
Write and persist a GraphQL query, then fetch it from React with the AEM headless client
Build React components that render Content Fragment data
Wire up the ui.frontend module and integrate the React build into AEM
Configure CORS and the dispatcher for cross-origin headless requests
Implement in-context editing with the SPA Editor / Universal Editor
Set up authentication tokens for fetching content in different environments
Configure Cloud Manager pipeline / local SDK quickstart for development

questions.question: Do you need in-context/visual editing or pure headless delivery?
How is the React app hosted relative to AEM?
How will React authenticate to AEM for content?
